title: Lovász–Nešetřil–Pultr Problem on product dimension of clique factors
statement_text: Estimate $Q(s, r)$, the product dimension of $K_s(r)$ (the vertex-disjoint union of $r$ copies of the complete graph $K_s$), for values of $s \geq 3$.
context_text: Lovász, Nešetřil and Pultr proved in 1980 that $Q(2,r) = \lceil \log_2(2r) \rceil$ and raised the problem of estimating $Q(s,r)$ for larger values of $s$. More recently, Kleinberg and Weinberg independently considered the same problem, motivated by prophet inequalities for intersections of matroids.
paper_contribution: Proves that for every fixed $s \geq 2$, $Q(s,r) = (1+o(1))\log_2 r$ where the $o(1)$-term tends to 0 as $r \to \infty$.
paper_title: On the product dimension of clique factors

title: Open Problem: asymptotic behavior of $Q(s,r)$ when $s$ and $r$ grow together
statement_text: Determine the asymptotic behavior of $Q(s, r)$ when $s$ and $r$ grow together (i.e., when $s$ is not fixed as $r \to \infty$).
context_text: The paper resolves $Q(s,r) = (1+o(1))\log_2 r$ for every fixed $s$ as $r \to \infty$, but explicitly states in the abstract that the problem of determining the asymptotic behavior of $Q(s,r)$ when both $s$ and $r$ grow simultaneously remains open.
notes: Section 4 (concluding remarks and open problems) is referenced in the introduction but its full content is not present in the extracted PDF text; additional open problems from that section may be missing.
paper_title: On the product dimension of clique factors
